title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
summary: MCP and ChatGPT apps use double iframe architecture to balance security and functionality. The outer iframe loads a lightweight script from a controlled subdomain, which then loads the actual app HTML via srcdoc into an inner frame, following a pattern Facebook pioneered for their app marketplace. This approach prevents both CSP blocking of third-party scripts and sandbox escapes while isolating per-app storage.
writeup: The talk explains the double iframe architecture behind MCP and ChatGPT apps, a design meant to balance security against functionality. The outer iframe loads a lightweight script from a controlled subdomain, which then injects the actual app HTML through srcdoc into an inner frame, a pattern Facebook originally developed for its app marketplace. The takeaway: the two-layer setup avoids Content Security Policy blocking of third-party scripts, blocks sandbox escapes, and isolates storage on a per-app basis, drawing on the same-origin policy and iframe sandboxing.
